conv_turns.content: Hi, I’m at the Atlanta airport and I just missed my flight to Boston. Can you help me get rebooked for today?
I can help with that. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the booking? Also, did you check any bags?
It’s K seven M D two Q, last name Patel. And yes, I checked one suitcase.

Thanks, Ms. Patel—I’ve pulled up K seven M D two Q, and I see the Atlanta to Boston flight has already departed. When a flight leaves, the reservation is typically marked as a no-show, and the system can lock the ticket from changes unless we reopen it. You’re still within our same-day grace window, so I can reinstate it and rebook you. Do you want the earliest flight out, or something later this afternoon?
Earliest, please. And I’m worried about my checked bag.

Okay, I’m seeing several options to Boston today. Crescent flight six eighty two at eleven forty a.m., nonstop, arriving two ten p.m., with no fare difference. Crescent flight four sixteen at two fifty five p.m., nonstop, arriving five thirty p.m., forty-eight dollars more. Crescent flight nine oh three at four twenty p.m. with a connection in Baltimore, arriving Boston at nine fifty five p.m., twenty dollars more. And Crescent flight two forty eight at seven thirty p.m., nonstop, arriving ten oh five p.m., sixty-nine dollars more. On the two earlier nonstops, economy is mostly middle seats right now, but I can still request an aisle if one opens up. Which one should I book?
Hi, I’m in Denver right now. I just got a notification that my flight to Portland tonight was canceled. Can you rebook me for tomorrow morning?
I can help with that. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
It’s J Q seven K nine P. Last name Patel.
Thanks, Mr. Patel. I see Sunbird flight S B four one two from Denver to Portland tonight at nine oh five PM is canceled due to weather. Are you able to travel tomorrow, and do you need nonstop only?
Yes—tomorrow morning, and nonstop if possible.

Okay. For tomorrow Denver to Portland, I’m seeing four rebooking options: one, S B two oh eight nonstop departing seven ten AM, arriving eight fifty five AM; two, S B six one four nonstop departing nine forty AM, arriving eleven twenty five AM; three, S B three nine zero departing one fifteen PM with a connection in Salt Lake City, arriving five forty PM with a one hour ten minute layover; and four, S B one four six nonstop departing six thirty PM, arriving eight fifteen PM.

Seat-wise, the seven ten has windows in rows nineteen and twenty, the nine forty has mostly middle seats left, the Salt Lake City connection has only back-of-plane seats, and the evening nonstop has a few aisles but it’s nearly full. Which option would you like?
Book the seven ten nonstop, please.

Done—you’re rebooked on S B two oh eight tomorrow, departing at seven ten AM and arriving Portland at eight fifty five AM. I’ve assigned seat nineteen A, and there’s no charge for the change. Do you also need a hotel and meal voucher for tonight?
Hi, I missed my flight this morning from Atlanta to San Francisco—traffic was awful. I’m at the airport now. Can you get me on something later today?
I can help. What’s your six-character confirmation code, and the last name on the reservation?
It’s R five K nine Q Z, last name Patel.
Thanks. I see your Atlanta to San Francisco booking for today, and it shows the flight already departed. I can check same-day rebooking options—are you trying to travel today?
Yes, today. I’m okay with a connection, but I need to arrive tonight.

Understood. Since you’re marked as a no-show, your original ticket can’t be reinstated as-is, so the way to travel today is a same-day “rescue” reissue. Here are the options I’m seeing right now: one, standby on the twelve oh five PM nonstop, flight November Whiskey one sixty, arriving two twenty-five PM, rescue fee seventy-five dollars, but you’d need to be at the gate by eleven twenty AM; two, confirmed at one ten PM to Phoenix on November Whiskey five oh two, then four twenty PM Phoenix to San Francisco on November Whiskey eight eighty-nine, arriving eight oh eight PM, total one hundred forty dollars; three, confirmed at three twenty PM to Denver on November Whiskey nine twenty-one, then six ten PM to San Francisco on November Whiskey six twelve, arriving nine oh five PM, total one hundred fifty dollars; four, confirmed at five forty-five PM to Dallas on November Whiskey three thirty, then seven fifty-five PM to San Francisco on November Whiskey seven seventy, arriving eleven fifty-eight PM, total one hundred twenty dollars. Which one would you like?
Let’s do the three twenty through Denver.

Okay—I’ll confirm you on the three twenty PM Atlanta to Denver, flight November Whiskey nine twenty-one, connecting to Denver to San Francisco at six ten PM on November Whiskey six twelve, arriving about nine oh five PM. The rescue reissue total is one hundred fifty dollars—should I charge the Visa ending in four two one eight?
Yes, charge that card.
All set—you’re confirmed and ticketed. Your new confirmation code is Foxtrot Juliett four Tango Mike Quebec: F J four T M Q. I’ve sent the updated itinerary by text and email.
Great. Can you put me on an aisle seat if there’s one?

Yes—I was able to get you aisle seats on both legs: seat fourteen C to Denver and seat twelve D to San Francisco. Now, let me go through all the details for you. Your first leg departs today at three twenty PM from gate B seven—boarding starts at two fifty PM with pre-boarding and first class, then Group one at two fifty-five, Group two at three PM, and general boarding at three oh five. You'll land in Denver at six forty-five PM local time, and your connecting gate is C twenty-two, which is about a ten-minute walk from the B concourse through the underground train. Your second leg departs Denver at seven thirty PM and arrives San Francisco around nine oh five PM Pacific time. Check-in is open in the app now, and if you're checking a bag, please have it dropped at least forty-five minutes before departure. The bag drop is on level two of the terminal near the check-in counters. Thanks for calling Northwind Airlines.
Hi, I’m flying tomorrow from Los Angeles to Honolulu, and I need to add one checked bag and a surfboard. Can you add that to my reservation?
Sure—I can help with that. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the booking?
It’s seven Kilo Quebec two Lima Juliet. Last name Vega.
Thanks. I found your booking, Marisol Vega, for tomorrow from Los Angeles to Honolulu. How many checked bags are you adding, and is the surfboard packed in a hard case?
Just one checked bag, and yes—the surfboard is in a hard case. It’s six foot eight.

Okay. Here’s how it works: your first checked bag is thirty-five dollars, and it can be up to fifty pounds. For the surfboard, we accept it as specialty sports equipment for seventy-five dollars each way as long as it’s in a hard-sided case; the case plus board can’t be over nine feet long or over seventy pounds. If it’s over those limits, oversize or overweight fees can apply on top, and space can be limited on certain aircraft. At the airport you’ll need to take it to the oversized bag counter and sign a limited-liability tag, and we recommend arriving at least ninety minutes before departure. I can add both now and charge the card ending in four one nine eight on your reservation—do you want me to do that?
Yes, charge the card on file. And can you move me to an aisle seat if there’s one available?

Got it—I’ve added one checked bag and one surfboard to your flight; the total is one hundred ten dollars. For an aisle seat, I can move you to seat fourteen D for ten dollars, or seat twenty-two C at no extra cost. Which one would you like?
I’ll take twenty-two C, the free one.

Done. You're now in seat twenty-two C, aisle, and your checked bag and surfboard are confirmed for tomorrow's Los Angeles to Honolulu flight. I've charged one hundred ten dollars to the card ending in four one nine eight—that's thirty-five dollars for the seat selection fee and seventy-five dollars for the oversized sporting equipment surcharge, which is separate from the standard checked bag fee you already paid. Now, for the surfboard specifically, there are some requirements: it needs to be in a padded board bag, the total length can't exceed nine feet six inches, and the weight limit is fifty pounds. If it's over fifty pounds there's an additional fifty-dollar overweight fee at the counter. Please drop the surfboard at the oversized bag counter at the airport—that's located at the far end of the check-in hall past counter forty, and they open at five AM. I'm sending the receipt to the email on file. Anything else you'd like to update today?
Hi, I just missed my seven fifteen AM flight from San Diego to Newark. The gate closed while I was running up. Can you get me on another flight today?
I’m sorry about that. I can pull up your booking—what’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
It’s Romeo six Mike Quebec two Zulu, last name Patel.
Thanks, Ms. Patel. I found your reservation for today’s San Diego to Newark flight, and it has already departed. Are you still at San Diego airport, and do you need to arrive in Newark today?
Yes, I’m at the airport now, and I really need to be in Newark tonight if possible.

Okay, so here’s the situation and the choices, just so you know what we’re working with. Since that flight has already left, the system marks you as a no-show, which means the original flight coupon is closed and—depending on the fare rules, like Basic versus Standard—your remaining value can be reduced or even forfeited unless we reissue it right away, and it also automatically cancels any onward segments if there were any.

What I can do is either: one, a same-day confirmed change, which comes with a same-day change fee of seventy-five dollars plus any fare difference, and that locks you onto a specific flight; or two, standby, which is usually free but it’s not guaranteed, it can only clear if seats open up close to departure, and you need to be at the gate at least forty minutes before takeoff, and if you checked bags we’d also have to reroute or intercept them so they don’t keep moving on the original itinerary.

For today from San Diego to Newark, I’m seeing four possibilities: flight one one eight nine at nine forty AM nonstop arriving six ten PM; flight two three one at eleven twenty AM with a connection in Denver to flight six zero two, arriving nine fifteen PM with about a one hour layover; flight nine zero seven at one fifty PM nonstop arriving ten thirty PM; and flight four one five at four thirty five PM with a connection in Chicago to flight eight four nine, arriving one twenty AM. I can also look at nearby airports like L A X or Orange County if you’re flexible, and there are upgrade choices like Economy Plus or First depending on seat availability.

Which one do you want to go with, and are you traveling with anyone else, any pets, any mobility assistance needs, and do you have any checked bags today?
Wow—okay. Book me on the nine forty AM nonstop in regular economy. No checked bags, just me.

Got it—I can confirm you on flight one one eight nine, departing at nine forty AM and arriving Newark at six ten PM. The same-day change fee is seventy-five dollars and the fare difference is twenty dollars, so the total is ninety-five dollars; I also have an aisle seat available in row twenty-two. Should I charge the card ending in four four two one?
Hi, I’m at Chicago O’Hare. My flight to Seattle tonight was cancelled and I need to get to Seattle tomorrow morning. Can you rebook me?
Yes, I can help. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
It’s K Q seven M V P, and the last name is Patel.

Thanks. I’ve pulled up reservation K Q seven M V P for Maya Patel. I see you arrived into Chicago from Indianapolis earlier today, and your Chicago O’Hare to Seattle Tacoma flight C A one ninety-seven, scheduled to depart six thirty P M from gate B twelve, was cancelled due to a mechanical issue with the aircraft. Since this is an airline-caused cancellation, change fees are waived and I can rebook you without collecting any fare difference.

Here are the rebooking options I’m showing right now:
Option one: tonight at nine forty-five P M, flight C A nine twenty-two, Chicago to Denver, then Denver to Seattle, arriving six fifty-five A M.
Option two: tonight at ten twenty P M, flight C A four thirty nonstop, arriving twelve twenty A M.
Option three: tomorrow at six oh-five A M, flight C A one fifty-one with a connection in Minneapolis, arriving ten forty A M.
Option four: tomorrow at seven fifty A M, flight C A two eighty-four nonstop, arriving ten ten A M.
Option five: tomorrow at nine thirty A M, flight C A six twelve with a connection in Salt Lake City, arriving one fifty P M.

Also, seats are limited—most of the nonstops only have middle seats left unless you purchase preferred seating—and I can transfer your one checked bag and your prepaid Wi‑Fi to the new itinerary. Which option would you like?
Book me on the seven fifty A M nonstop. And since I’m stuck overnight, can you help with a hotel voucher?

I can check hotel coverage for you. Since the cancellation is mechanical, you’re generally eligible for a hotel voucher when you’re away from your home city, but it can still depend on things like whether Chicago is your final destination, whether you have a local address within about fifty miles, and whether we still have rooms available with our contracted hotels tonight.

The voucher covers the room and tax only—parking, meals, room service, and any incidentals aren’t included—and the hotel will usually require a credit card at check-in for a security deposit. If you choose to book your own hotel instead, we can sometimes review reimbursement, but only with an itemized receipt, and it may take two to three weeks.

To set this up: is it just you traveling, do you need an accessible room, and what email address should I send the voucher to?
Just me, no accessible room. Email is maya dot patel at gmail dot com. And will my checked bag be okay? It’s already tagged to Seattle.

Thanks. I’ve rebooked you on flight C A two eighty-four tomorrow at seven fifty A M, nonstop to Seattle, and your confirmation code stays K Q seven M V P. I’ve assigned seat twenty-two A for now, and your checked bag will transfer automatically.

I’m emailing your hotel voucher now—voucher number H T L dash seven six four nine. Would you like a meal voucher as well?
Yes, please. And can you move me to an aisle closer to the front if possible? I don’t want to pay extra.

Okay—I'm issuing a meal voucher for fifteen dollars. The code is M E A L dash P A T E L dash seven four eight; it’s valid until midnight Central time, it works at most participating food vendors in terminals one, two, and three, and it won’t cover alcohol or gratuity.

For aisle seats on tomorrow’s flight, at no charge I’m seeing row fourteen C, row sixteen C, row eighteen D, row twenty C, and row twenty-four D. If you’re open to paid upgrades, extra-legroom aisle seats nine C or ten D are forty-nine dollars, and first class aisle seat two D is one hundred ninety dollars. Which seat should I lock in?
Hi, my flight tonight from Raleigh-Durham to Denver just got canceled in the app. I’m at the airport—can you rebook me, and do I get a hotel?
I can help. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
It’s Juliet Quebec four Romeo Tango Zulu, last name Vega.
Thanks—your Raleigh-Durham to Denver flight tonight is showing as canceled due to weather. Would you like me to rebook you, or cancel the trip and request a refund?
Please rebook. I need to be in Denver before noon tomorrow, and I already checked one bag.

Got it—I’ll look for options that get you into Denver before noon. Since this cancellation is weather-related, I can rebook you at no charge, but hotel and meal vouchers generally aren’t issued for weather disruptions. If you have trip insurance or a credit card with trip delay coverage, it may reimburse those expenses. Do you want a nonstop even if it leaves later, or the earliest departure?
Earliest arrival before noon, please—I’ll sort out the hotel myself.

I have two that arrive before noon: a six twenty AM flight with one stop in Dallas, arriving Denver at eleven thirty AM, or a nonstop at eight ten AM arriving at ten fifteen AM. Which one should I book?
Hi, I’m at Chicago O’Hare. My flight from Raleigh was late and I missed my connection to Seattle. Can you rebook me?
I can help with that. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
It’s G H five nine P Z, last name Morales.
Thanks—just to confirm: G as in Golf, H as in Hotel, five, nine, P as in Papa, Z as in Zulu, for Morales. Is that correct?
Yes, that’s right.

Okay, I’ve pulled up your trip for Ethan Morales: flight four oh seven from Raleigh-Durham to Chicago O’Hare today, and you were connecting to flight eighteen twelve to Seattle, which has already departed.

To get you to Seattle, I’m seeing four options: one, depart at seven forty five PM to Denver then connect at nine thirty PM to Seattle, arriving at eleven twenty PM; two, depart at ten fifteen PM to Minneapolis then connect at six oh five AM, arriving at eight fifteen AM; three, a nonstop tomorrow at seven thirty AM arriving at nine forty five AM; or four, a nonstop tomorrow at one fifty PM arriving at four ten PM. The first two are even exchange, the morning nonstop is twenty nine dollars more, and the one fifty is the same price. Which one do you want?
Hi, I need to cancel my flight to Toronto next Tuesday and get a refund. I booked it with points.
Sure—I can help. What’s your six-character confirmation code, and the last name on the reservation?
It’s Kilo Quebec Tango nine seven two. Last name Patel.

Thanks. I’ve pulled up confirmation K Q T nine seven two for Priya Patel. You’re booked on flight U A one eight three from Newark to Toronto Pearson on Tuesday, March seventeenth, departing six ten PM and arriving seven forty-five PM, economy saver award.

Just so you know, with award tickets we can either cancel and redeposit the miles, refund the taxes back to the original card, or keep the value as a future flight credit if you’d rather move the trip; if you’re within twenty-four hours of booking we can do a full refund automatically, and if you’re canceling due to illness you can also submit a medical waiver request for any nonrefundable portions, though approvals aren’t guaranteed. Also, if you purchased any add-ons—like preferred seating, an Economy Plus upgrade, a checked bag, or Wi‑Fi—those can have separate refund timelines and sometimes take up to two billing cycles.

Before I process this, can you confirm whether you want to cancel just the outbound or an entire round trip including a return, whether any part of the itinerary has already been flown, and whether you want the miles redeposited versus held as a credit?
Hi, I missed my flight this morning from Atlanta to San Diego. Can you help me get on another flight today?
I can help with that. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
The code is Tango Romeo seven five Kilo November, and the last name is Coleman.

Thanks, Darius. I see you were booked on Pioneer Airlines flight Papa November five two eight from Atlanta to San Diego, scheduled to depart at eight ten AM today. Are you still at Atlanta, and are you trying to get to San Diego today?
Yes, I’m at the airport now. I checked one bag, and I really need to be in San Diego tonight.

Okay, so, just to set expectations, because that flight has already departed and you were marked as a no-show, your original ticket isn’t active anymore, and we have to either reinstate it with a missed-flight fee or price a brand-new ticket, and the cheapest route depends on what’s still open right now. Also, since you checked a bag, it’s typically pulled and held at the Atlanta baggage service office; if we rebook you quickly we can usually retag it to the new flight, but if there’s a longer gap you may need to pick it up and recheck it, which can add thirty to forty five minutes.

For Atlanta to San Diego today, I’m seeing several choices: option one, nonstop Papa November six one two leaving ten fifty AM and arriving one twenty PM, but it’s mostly middle seats and would be about two hundred ninety dollars more; option two, nonstop Papa November six four eight leaving one forty PM arriving four fifteen PM, about one hundred sixty dollars more; option three, nonstop Papa November seven zero four leaving six fifteen PM arriving eight ten PM, about one hundred forty two dollars more; option four, one stop via Phoenix—Papa November five nine eight at twelve twenty PM to Phoenix, then Papa November eight one two to San Diego—getting in around five fifty PM, about one hundred ten dollars more; and option five, one stop via Dallas—Papa November six three six at three oh five PM to Dallas, then Papa November nine four four to San Diego—arriving around nine thirty PM, about ninety five dollars more. On top of that, boarding doors close about fifteen minutes before departure, so earlier flights can be tight.

Which one do you want, and do you want me to book it as confirmed or try standby first?
Let’s do the six fifteen PM nonstop, and confirmed is fine.

Got it—Pioneer flight Papa November seven zero four, Atlanta to San Diego, departing six fifteen PM and arriving eight ten PM. The total due is one hundred forty two dollars. Should I charge the Visa ending in four zero one six?
Hi, I'm at Minneapolis Saint Paul right now and I just missed my flight to Las Vegas. Can you get me on the next one?
I can help. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
It’s Papa Kilo seven four Romeo Tango, last name Calderon.
Thanks, Renee Calderon. I see you were booked on Northbridge flight N B three fifty-eight from Minneapolis to Las Vegas this morning. What’s the latest you can arrive today?
I really need to be there today—earlier is better. And I checked one bag about an hour ago.
Understood. I can put you on standby for a ten fifty AM nonstop, or confirm you on the three thirty PM nonstop for a fare difference of ninety-two dollars. Which do you want?
Please confirm the three thirty. Am I going to get hit with a no-show fee for missing the first one?

There isn’t a separate no-show fee. Since the flight already departed, the system tags it as a no-show and it can cancel the rest automatically, so I’m reinstating your ticket as we speak. I’ll lock in that three thirty PM seat for you now.
Hi, I’m at Sea-Tac. My flight to Chicago just left and I missed it. Can you help me get rebooked for today?
I can help with a missed-flight rebooking. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
It’s K seven M Q T Z, last name Patel.

Thanks. I found K seven M Q T Z for Jordan Patel—Cobalt flight two twenty one from Seattle to Chicago O’Hare, scheduled to depart at eight ten AM, and it’s marked as a no-show. Are you trying to get to O’Hare today, and do you want nonstop only?
Yes, O’Hare today. Nonstop if possible. I was hoping to land before four.

Okay—just so you know, with the time zone change, none of the remaining Seattle to O’Hare flights today arrive before four PM Chicago time. Here are the choices I’m seeing: nonstop Cobalt flight five twelve at eleven twenty AM arriving five fifteen PM, middle seats only; nonstop flight five sixteen at two oh five PM arriving seven fifty PM, with one aisle seat left; one-stop via Minneapolis, flight nine oh four at twelve forty PM connecting to three thirty one, arriving six fifty five PM, about sixty dollars cheaper but a fifty-minute connection; or the last nonstop tonight, flight five twenty four at ten forty five PM arriving five thirty AM tomorrow. Because the original flight has already departed, the system adds a missed-flight reissue fee of one hundred fifty dollars plus any fare difference. Which one do you want?
Book me on the two oh five nonstop.

All right. The two oh five PM nonstop, flight five sixteen, is available. The missed-flight reissue fee is one hundred fifty dollars and today’s fare difference is twenty eight dollars, so the total is one hundred seventy eight dollars. Should I charge the card on file ending four four eight one?
Yes, charge that one. And please text me the boarding pass.

Payment approved. You’re confirmed on Cobalt flight five sixteen departing Seattle at two oh five PM and arriving Chicago O’Hare at seven fifty PM, and your confirmation code stays K seven M Q T Z. Your current seat is twenty one D, and I’ve texted your boarding pass.
Hi, I'm trying to upgrade my seat on my New York to Rome flight in April.
Sure. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the booking?
It’s Romeo seven Kilo nine Mike Papa, last name Ortiz.

Thanks. I found Daniela Ortiz on Lumen Air flight five eight two from New York J F K to Rome Fiumicino on Tuesday, April twenty second, departing at six fifty PM. You’re currently in Main Cabin seat twenty nine B. Premium Economy is available for three hundred eighty dollars, and Business is one thousand two hundred. Which would you like?
Premium Economy, please—and I’d prefer an aisle.

Okay, in Premium Economy I’m seeing aisle seats in row thirteen seats C and D, row fourteen seats C and D, row fifteen seat C, row sixteen seats C and D, and row seventeen seat C. Window seats are thirteen A, fourteen A, fifteen A, sixteen A, and seventeen A. Row thirteen is the bulkhead, so there’s no under-seat storage and the tray tables are in the armrests. Which seat number do you want me to assign?
Let’s do fifteen C.
Great—fifteen C is available. The Premium Economy upgrade is three hundred eighty dollars. Want me to purchase it now?
Yes, please. Also, can you add one extra checked bag and request a gluten-free meal?

I can do that. An additional checked bag on this itinerary is sixty dollars, and the gluten-free meal request is free. I’ll add both and charge a total of four hundred forty dollars to the card on file ending in two one four eight—does that work?
Yes, that works.

All set. You're upgraded to Premium Economy on flight five eight two on April twenty second, and your seat is now fifteen C. I added one extra checked bag and a gluten-free meal request, and charged four hundred forty dollars to the card ending in two one four eight. Let me tell you what's included with Premium Economy so you know what to expect: you get priority boarding in Group two, a wider seat with six inches of extra legroom, a premium meal service with complimentary wine and beer, two free checked bags—so with the extra one I added you now have three total—priority baggage handling so your bags come out first, and access to the express security lane at participating airports. For the gluten-free meal, it'll be served about an hour into the flight, and it's a pre-set menu so you won't choose from the cart, but it's prepared by the same catering team. I'm sending the updated receipt and itinerary to the email on your reservation. Anything else I can help with today?
Hi, I’m at Chicago O’Hare. My flight to Newark tonight got canceled, and I need to get home tomorrow morning. Can you rebook me?
I can help with that. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the booking?
It’s Romeo Papa four seven November Charlie, last name Hawkins.

Thanks. I found your reservation for Jordan Hawkins on Lakefront Air flight Lima Foxtrot two one seven from Chicago O’Hare to Newark tonight at nine ten P M, and it was canceled due to an aircraft maintenance issue. I can rebook you on the next available flight or cancel it for a refund—what would you like to do?
Please rebook me. What’s the earliest you can get me into Newark tomorrow, and do I get a hotel for tonight?

Okay, I’m showing several choices for tomorrow from O’Hare to Newark. Option one: six oh five A M nonstop, flight Lima Foxtrot one zero three, arriving nine oh eight A M, but it’s main cabin with only middle seats left. Option two: seven thirty A M with one stop in Detroit—Lima Foxtrot four eight two to Detroit, then Lima Foxtrot nine one seven to Newark—arriving eleven forty A M, with aisle seats available but about a ninety-minute connection. Option three: nine fifteen A M nonstop, flight Lima Foxtrot two zero nine, arriving twelve ten P M, aisle or window available, but there’s a forty-two dollar fare difference. Option four: eleven fifty A M with one stop in Cleveland—Lima Foxtrot three one four then Lima Foxtrot eight zero two—arriving four oh five P M, no fare difference. Since this cancellation is maintenance-related, you’re also eligible for a one-night hotel voucher and a meal voucher, and I can issue those once we pick a flight. Which option do you want?
Book me on the six oh five nonstop. Middle seat is fine.

Done. You’re rebooked on Lakefront Air flight Lima Foxtrot one zero three tomorrow, departing O’Hare at six oh five A M and arriving Newark at nine oh eight A M. Seat twenty two B is assigned, and there’s no change fee or fare difference. I’m sending the updated boarding pass to the mobile number ending in one six two four—should I also email it?
Hi, I missed my flight this morning from Austin to Seattle. I’m at the airport now—can you help me get on another flight today?
I can help. What’s your six-character confirmation code and the last name on the reservation?
It’s Golf Sierra seven two Papa Mike, last name Nguyen.
Thanks, Mr. Nguyen. I’ve pulled up your Austin to Seattle reservation. What’s the latest you can arrive today?
Before nine tonight, and I’m okay with one connection.

Okay, here are the same-day options I’m seeing. Option one is nonstop: departing Austin at eleven fifty A M on flight Charlie Romeo two sixteen, arriving Seattle at two thirty-five P M, total to rebook two hundred sixty-eight dollars, middle seats only. Option two leaves at one ten P M to Denver on Charlie Romeo three thirty-eight, connects at three fifty P M on Charlie Romeo seven eleven, gets into Seattle at six thirty P M, total two hundred five dollars, about an hour and twenty minutes to connect. Option three is three fifteen P M to Phoenix on Charlie Romeo one zero four, then five ten P M Phoenix to Seattle on Charlie Romeo five twenty, arriving seven fifty-five P M, total one hundred sixty dollars, with a forty-five minute connection. Option four is two twenty-five P M to Las Vegas on Charlie Romeo four sixty, then four fifty P M to Seattle on Charlie Romeo eight oh two, arriving seven thirty-five P M, total one hundred ninety-nine dollars, and the second leg only has window seats. Option five is four fifty P M to San Francisco on Charlie Romeo five seventy-two, then six forty P M to Seattle on Charlie Romeo nine sixteen, arriving eight fifty-five P M, total one hundred seventy-five dollars, with a two-hour layover. Which one would you like?
Hi, I’m at Boston Logan and I just missed my flight to Phoenix this morning. I really need to get there today—can you rebook me?
I can help with that. What’s your six-character confirmation code, and the last name on the reservation?
Code is K seven P Q two L. Last name Patel.
Thanks, I found it. Are you traveling with any checked bags today?
No, just a carry-on.

Okay—your original flight two seventy-nine from Boston to Phoenix was scheduled at seven ten AM and it’s already departed, so the system marks it as a missed departure. Here are the same-day choices I can offer right now. One: standby for the next nonstop, flight six twenty-three at ten forty-five AM, arriving Phoenix at two oh five PM; the standby fee is thirty-five dollars, and there are only middle seats showing, plus you’d need to get to gate C nine in about twenty minutes. Two: a confirmed itinerary at one twenty PM connecting in Minneapolis, arriving seven fifty PM, for ninety-eight dollars more. Three: a confirmed itinerary at three fifteen PM connecting in Denver, arriving about eight ten PM, for eighty-two dollars more. Four: a nine fifty PM nonstop arriving twelve twenty AM, for one hundred forty dollars more. I can also check tomorrow morning if you want the cheapest fare, but you’d be traveling a day later. Which option should I lock in?
